name: ENANTA PHARMACEUTICALS INC
business: Enanta Pharmaceuticals is a biotechnology company that discovers and develops small molecule drugs for virology and immunology indications. It generates royalty revenue through a collaboration with AbbVie for hepatitis C virus treatments, primarily MAVYRET/MAVIRET. The company also has internal programs targeting respiratory syncytial virus, SARS-CoV-2, hepatitis B, and type 2 inflammatory diseases.
sellsTo: Patients with acute or chronic hepatitis C virus infection
Infants, young children, adults over 65, and individuals with comorbidities at risk for RSV
Patients with chronic spontaneous urticaria or atopic dermatitis

exposures.sentence: We are a biotechnology company that uses our robust, chemistry-driven approach and drug discovery capabilities to discover and develop small molecule drugs for virology and immunology indications.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of MAVYRET for acute HCV infection
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025 it was also approved by the FDA as the first and only treatment for acute HCV infection.
the proceeds from our public offering in October 2025

tamClaims.claim: RSV burden in the U.S.: up to 6.5 million outpatient visits, 350,000 hospitalizations, and 23,000 deaths annually
tamClaims.sentence: Recent CDC estimates suggest a significant RSV burden in the U.S., with up to 6.5 million outpatient visits, 350,000 hospitalizations and 23,000 deaths annually.

tamClaims.claim: Chronic spontaneous urticaria: global prevalence 0.5%-1% of population, approximately 1.75-3.5 million people in U.S. alone
tamClaims.sentence: Chronic spontaneous urticaria, or CSU, a severely debilitating, chronic inflammatory skin disease manifested by hives, angioedema, which is swelling of soft tissues, or both, but with no identified triggers, which has an estimated global prevalence of between 0.5% 1% of the population, resulting in approximately 1.75 - 3.5 million people with this condition at any given time in the U.S. alone

tamClaims.claim: Atopic dermatitis affects 7.3% of US adult population, ~40% moderate to severe
tamClaims.sentence: AD affecting 7.3% of the US adult population, of whom ~40% have moderate to severe disease.
